SEARCH

Class MyPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>>

java.lang.Object
MyPriorityQueue<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the element type, must be Comparable
Direct Known Subclasses:
MyClassicPriorityQueue
public class MyPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>> extends Object
A generic min-heap priority queue backed by an ArrayList. T must be Comparable so elements can be ordered naturally. The heap is stored in a zero-indexed array layout where the parent of index i is at (i - 1) / 2 and the children of index i are at 2i + 1 (left) and 2i + 2 (right).
Version:
4/28
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected ArrayList<T>
    heap
     

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MyPriorityQueue()
    Constructs an empty priority queue.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    add(T t)
    Inserts t at the end of the heap then bubbles it up to restore the heap property.
    protected void
    bubbleUp(int index)
    Moves the element at the given index upward until its parent is no larger than it, restoring the heap property above the index.
    boolean
    isEmpty()
    Returns true if the heap contains no elements.
    boolean
    isHeap()
    Iteratively verifies the heap ordering property for every element by checking that each node is no larger than its left and right children.
    T
    peek()
    Returns the minimal element without removing it from the heap.
    T
    poll()
    Removes and returns the minimal element (the root of the heap).
    protected void
    siftDown(int index)
    Moves the element at the given index downward until both of its children are no smaller than it, restoring the heap property below the index.
    int
    size()
    Returns the number of elements currently in the heap.
    protected void
    swap(int i, int j)
    Swaps the elements at positions i and j in the heap.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Field Details

    • heap

      protected ArrayList<T extends Comparable<T>> heap

  • Constructor Details

    • MyPriorityQueue

      public MyPriorityQueue()
      Constructs an empty priority queue.

  • Method Details

    • add

      public boolean add(T t)
      Inserts t at the end of the heap then bubbles it up to restore the heap property.
      Parameters:
      t - the element to insert
      Returns:
      true always, indicating the element was added

    • bubbleUp

      protected void bubbleUp(int index)
      Moves the element at the given index upward until its parent is no larger than it, restoring the heap property above the index.
      Parameters:
      index - the starting index of the element to bubble up

    • poll

      public T poll()
      Removes and returns the minimal element (the root of the heap). The last element is moved to the root and sifted down to restore the heap property.
      Returns:
      the minimal element, or null if the queue is empty

    • siftDown

      protected void siftDown(int index)
      Moves the element at the given index downward until both of its children are no smaller than it, restoring the heap property below the index.
      Parameters:
      index - the starting index of the element to sift down

    • peek

      public T peek()
      Returns the minimal element without removing it from the heap.
      Returns:
      the minimal element, or null if the queue is empty

    • isHeap

      public boolean isHeap()
      Iteratively verifies the heap ordering property for every element by checking that each node is no larger than its left and right children.
      Returns:
      true if the heap property holds for every node, false otherwise

    • swap

      protected void swap(int i, int j)
      Swaps the elements at positions i and j in the heap. Subclasses override this to also update any auxiliary data structures.
      Parameters:
      i - the first index
      j - the second index

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of elements currently in the heap.
      Returns:
      the size of the heap

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if the heap contains no elements.
      Returns:
      true if empty,false otherwise
